Sources.Title: Jordan Syatt — 30 Days Of Sugar: My Blood Test Results
Jordan Syatt — Me, Myself, and I: Jordan Syatt in the Past, Present, and Future (readable mirror)

TranscriptionNotes: Jordan Syatt's own channel publishes his comprehensive before-and-after blood panel. The September 2023 column visibly provides all nine stored PhenoAge inputs: hs-CRP at 1:40, glucose at 1:45, albumin and alkaline phosphatase at 1:48–1:49, creatinine at 1:50, lymphocyte percentage at 1:55, and MCV, RDW, and WBC at 1:57.
The panel labels the accepted draw only as September 2023, so Date uses the first day of that month with DatePrecision='Month'; no collection day is invented.
Jordan's dated first-party birthday post establishes that he was 32 throughout September 2023. AgeYears preserves that whole-year age without republishing or encoding a date of birth.
The birthday post is linked through a read-only HTTPS mirror because the origin site blocks automated requests; the first-party origin URL remains explicit in the mirror path.
The panel reports hs-CRP as below 0.3 mg/L. The stored value is the published assay limit with a '<' qualifier, not an exact measured concentration.
Albumin was converted from 4.6 g/dL to 46 g/L by multiplying by 10.
Creatinine was converted from 1.25 mg/dL to 110.5 µmol/L by multiplying by 88.4.
Glucose was converted from 78 mg/dL to 4.329 mmol/L by multiplying by 0.0555.
